title: Transparent Scoreboard by Akis
description: A simple core shader that removes the background of scoreboards
body:
<details>
<summary>❗[Notice] Google will lock-down Android and your ability to install applications❗</summary>

Starting in September 2026, Android will require all apps to be registered by verified developers in order to be installed on certified Android devices, and Google gets to decide who 'verified developers' are.

- Your right to <u>freely</u> develop and **install** whatever apps (`.apk` files) you want may cease.
- App developers will be forced to hand over govt. ids to Google and tie their apps to their real life identities.
- Google gets to decide who gets approved. Being rejected means nobody can install your apps.
- Developers Google arbirarily chooses it doesn't like, as well as users who reside in sanctioned regions like Russia, may face difficulties or be cut-off entirely from having their apps installable.

Please visit the following resources for more infromation:
- [Official Google announcement and article](https://developer.android.com/developer-verification)
- [Keep Android Open (.org)](https://keepandroidopen.org/)
- [Consumer Rights Wiki article](https://consumerrights.wiki/w/Android_Developer_Verification)

</details>

# Transparent Scoreboard Background

## Overview
This simple yet effective texture pack removes the background of the scoreboard in Minecraft, providing a cleaner and more immersive HUD experience. Perfect for players who prefer a minimalist interface or want to reduce on-screen clutter.

## Installation
1. Download the texture pack.
2. Place the pack in your `resourcepacks` folder.
3. Enable the pack in Minecraft's Resource Packs menu.
4. Enjoy a cleaner scoreboard display!

## Notes
- This pack is designed for vanilla Minecraft and may not work with Mods or some Shaders
- The Lunar Client mod "Scoreboard" overrides how this works, meaning this won't work for players using it.
